summary: Explained why the DinoBrain data repo was not committed in the previous turn: the repo was already ahead of origin and had a large mixed dirty set of modified/generated candidate and review artifacts, so a broad commit would have bypassed review and sync policy.
decisions: Keep data repo commits scoped to reviewed/sync-policy-approved paths instead of committing the entire dirty worktree.
next_steps: Run a scoped data sync or reviewed commit plan for the two ahead commits and the current 1586 dirty data paths before pushing dinobrain-data.
